title: Find Relief from Heartburn with These 10 Herbal Teas
intro_desc: Herbal teas for heartburn are natural remedies that help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with acid reflux and indigestion. They work by reducing inflammation, soothing the digestive tract, and promoting relaxation. The best herbal teas for heartburn include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinchona officinalis, Mentha x piperita, Althaea officinalis, Aloe ferox, Urtica dioica, Matricaria chamomilla, and Aloe barbadensis. If left untreated, heartburn can disrupt daily life, causing sleep disturbances, anxiety, and digestive issues, ultimately affecting one's overall quality of life and well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy for heartburn. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the esophagus and stomach, which can cause heartburn. When you drink ginger tea, the warmth and soothing properties of ginger calm the digestive system and ease digestion. This can help prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus, which is a common cause of heartburn. Additionally, ginger tea can also help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help food move through the digestive system more smoothly. This can also help reduce the amount of acid produced in the stomach, further alleviating heartburn symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for heartburn because they help calm down the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can cause the burning sensation in the chest and throat associated with heartburn.

Shogaols are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Gingerol is good for heartburn because it helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and relaxes the muscles that cause stomach acid to flow up into the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for heartburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal teas that help with heartburn, start by gathering dried nettle leaves, as Urtica dioica is also known as stinging nettle. If you have fresh nettle leaves, dry them first in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. Next, combine one teaspoon of dried nettle leaves with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or mug. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the herbs' healing properties. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to use it in moderation as the tea already has a natural soothing effect on the digestive system. Drinking this tea regularly can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn by reducing inflammation and calming the stomach muscles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, can help with heartburn because it has soothing properties that calm the digestive system. The main compound in licorice root, glycyrrhizin, reduces inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, which can cause heartburn. This inflammation can be caused by stomach acid coming up into the esophagus, and glycyrrhizin helps to neutralize the acid and prevent further irritation. Additionally, licorice root tea can help to relax the muscles in the stomach, which can also contribute to heartburn. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the stomach muscles, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea can provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with heartburn.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for heartburn because it helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and relax the muscles that cause stomach acid to flow back up into the throat.

Polyphenols are good for heartburn because they help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, prevent acid production, and have antioxidant properties that soothe and protect the esophagus and stomach lining.

Flavonoids are good for heartburn because they help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, prevent the production of acid, and soothe the esophagus, providing relief from the burning sensation associated with heartburn.
Tannins are good for heartburn because they help to reduce the amount of acid in the stomach and soothe the lining, thereby easing the burning sensation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
Saponins are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the stomach lining from acid damage, reducing symptoms of heartburn and inflammation.

remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for heartburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal teas that help with heartburn, start by gathering fresh or dried Urtica dioica leaves, also known as stinging nettle. If using fresh leaves, pick them from a clean and pesticide-free area and rinse them with water to remove any dirt. Next, chop the leaves into small pieces and place them in a heat-resistant container. For dried leaves, simply add one teaspoon of dried nettle to a cup. Pour boiling water over the leaves, making sure to cover them completely, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and add honey or lemon to taste. Urtica dioica has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system and alleviate heartburn symptoms. You can consume this herbal tea 2-3 times a day to get relief from heartburn. However, it is recommended to consult a healthcare professional before using Urtica dioica as a treatment for heartburn, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare tea, also known as fennel tea, is a natural remedy that can help soothe heartburn. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and volatile oils, have anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. When you drink fennel tea, it can help relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce the pressure that can cause stomach acid to flow back up into the throat. This can bring quick relief from heartburn symptoms, such as a burning sensation in the chest and throat. Fennel tea also has a mild antacid effect, which can help neutralize stomach acid and provide long-lasting relief from heartburn. Additionally, fennel tea may also help improve digestion and reduce symptoms of bloating and gas, which can contribute to heartburn.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anethole helps with heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.

Polyphenols are good for heartburn because they help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, which prevents stomach acid from flowing back up into the throat and causing discomfort.
Foeniculin is good for heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the lining of the esophagus, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause heartburn.

remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal teas for heartburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal tea for heartburn relief, start by gathering dried nettle leaves, which can be found in health food stores or online. Begin by boiling a cup of water, then add a teaspoon of dried nettle leaves to the water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their healing properties. After the steeping time, strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. You can add honey to sweeten the tea if needed. Some people find that drinking Urtica dioica tea on an empty stomach can help reduce heartburn symptoms, but you may want to try it before meals to see what works best for you. Drinking a cup of this tea after meals or before bedtime may also help soothe digestive issues and promote a restful night's sleep.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tea is a natural remedy that can help with heartburn. This tea is made from the bark of the Cinchona tree, which has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. The main reason Cinchona officinalis tea is good for heartburn is that it contains compounds that help reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus. This inflammation is often the cause of heartburn, a burning sensation in the chest. By reducing inflammation, the tea helps to ease the discomfort and pain associated with heartburn. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis tea has antacid properties, which help neutralize stomach acid and reduce the symptoms of heartburn. It also has a soothing effect on the digestive system, which can help calm the stomach and prevent heartburn from occurring in the first place. The tea's natural ingredients work together to provide relief from heartburn symptoms and promote a healthier digestive system.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quinic acid is good for heartburn because it helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and soothes the stomach lining, preventing acid from flowing up into the esophagus and causing discomfort.
Tannins help reduce heartburn by soothing the stomach lining and reducing inflammation, which in turn allows for better digestion and relief from burning sensations.
Quercetin is good for heartburn because it helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and calm down the digestive system, preventing acid from flowing back up into the throat.

remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal teas for heartburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal teas that help with heartburn, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves of the plant, also known as stinging nettle. Choose clean and dry equipment, like a saucepan and strainer, to avoid contamination. If using fresh leaves, rinse them gently with cold water to remove dirt. Place the nettle leaves in the saucepan, add 1 cup of boiling water, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the plant's properties to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. For dried nettle leaves, follow the same steps but adjust the steeping time to 3-5 minutes. This herbal tea is believed to help alleviate heartburn symptoms by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and promoting digestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint tea, is a popular choice for heartburn relief. The main reason for this is that peppermint tea helps to relax the muscles in the esophagus and the lower esophageal sphincter, which is a muscle that separates the esophagus and the stomach. When the lower esophageal sphincter is relaxed, stomach acid can flow back up into the esophagus, causing heartburn. Peppermint tea helps to calm this muscle and prevent stomach acid from flowing back up, reducing heartburn symptoms. Additionally, peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the esophagus and further alleviate heartburn symptoms. When consumed after meals, peppermint tea can also help to speed up digestion and reduce the amount of time that stomach acid is in contact with the esophagus, further reducing heartburn symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for heartburn because it helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and relaxes the muscles, which can ease symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Quercetin is good for heartburn because it helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and relaxes the muscles that allow stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Menthol is good for heartburn because it helps to relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce inflammation, making it easier to swallow and relieving symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Tannins are good for heartburn because they help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and also slow down the movement of food into the stomach, giving the body more time to digest it properly and preventing acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.

remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for heartburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal tea to help with heartburn, start by gathering fresh or dried nettle leaves, which is the other name for Urtica dioica. If using fresh leaves, pick them from a trusted source and rinse them with cold water to remove any dirt. If using dried leaves, you can find them at a health food store or online. Next, bring a pot of water to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add one to two teaspoons of the nettle leaves to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their medicinal properties. Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste, and drink the tea after meals to help soothe heartburn symptoms. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have a severe heartburn condition or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender tea, has been used for centuries to help with heartburn. This is because it contains compounds that help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, which can become overactive and cause heartburn. When these muscles are relaxed, they don't contract as much, allowing food to move through the digestive system more easily. This can also help to reduce inflammation and spasms in the esophagus, which are common causes of heartburn. Additionally, the antioxidants in Lavandula angustifolia tea may help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, further reducing the symptoms of heartburn. By promoting digestion and reducing inflammation, this tea can provide relief from heartburn and other digestive issues.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the esophagus and reduce acid reflux.
Linalyl acetate has a calming effect on the digestive system which can help reduce symptoms of heartburn by relaxing the muscles in the esophagus and reducing inflammation.
Rosmarinic acid is good for heartburn because it helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can cause heartburn symptoms to decrease.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal teas that help with heartburn, start by gathering a few fresh or dried nettle leaves from a trusted source. You can find these leaves in health food stores or grow them in your own garden. Next, rinse the nettle leaves in cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, place one teaspoon of the leaves in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag, depending on your preference. Add one cup of boiling water to the infuser or bag, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to release their active ingredients. Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice to enhance the flavor and benefits. You can enjoy this tea warm or at room temperature, up to 3 times a day, to help soothe heartburn symptoms and support digestive health. Be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using Urtica dioica herbal tea, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or take medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is a popular remedy for heartburn. It contains compounds that help soothe and calm the stomach lining. The anti-inflammatory properties in aloe barbadensis reduce swelling in the esophagus, which can cause heartburn. This swelling can be due to stomach acid flowing back up into the esophagus. Aloe barbadensis tea also has a natural cooling effect, which can provide quick relief from the burning sensation of heartburn. Additionally, aloe barbadensis contains prebiotic fibers that help regulate the balance of good bacteria in the gut, which can aid in digestion and reduce symptoms of heartburn. Drinking aloe barbadensis tea may also help reduce the production of stomach acid, further alleviating heartburn symptoms.

Flavonoids are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the esophagus and ease digestion, thereby providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Aloin is good for heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the production of stomach acid and soothe the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal tea for heartburn relief, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves and stems of the plant, also known as nettle. If using fresh, simply rinse the leaves and stems with water to clean them, and then chop them into small pieces. If using dried, you can skip this step. Next, place one to two teaspoons of the chopped nettle into a tea infuser or directly into a saucepan. Pour in one cup of boiling water over the nettle, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water. Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste if needed. Finally, drink the tea hot or cold, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate heartburn symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile tea, is a popular remedy for heartburn. The tea's soothing properties help calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the esophagus. Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, work to ease acid reflux symptoms by reducing the production of stomach acid and relaxing the muscles in the esophagus. Additionally, chamomile tea's natural antispasmodic properties help to calm down muscle spasms in the digestive tract, preventing heartburn symptoms from worsening. By promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, chamomile tea can also help to reduce stress, which is a common trigger for heartburn. Drinking chamomile tea after meals can help to neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from heartburn symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Apigenin is good for heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and prevent the occurrence of acid reflux.

Luteolin may be good for heartburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause heartburn symptoms.
Polyphenols have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract and reduce acid production, thereby providing relief from heartburn symptoms.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal teas that help with heartburn, start by gathering fresh or dried leaves and flowers of the plant. You can find Urtica dioica in health food stores or grow it yourself in a garden. If using fresh leaves, rinse them with clean water and pat them dry. Then, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or mug. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties. Once the mixture has cooled slightly, strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth. You can add a sweetener like honey if desired, but be sure to drink it warm, as hot or iced tea may not be as effective. Drinking 1-2 cups of Urtica dioica tea per day may help alleviate heartburn symptoms and soothe digestive issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cynara scolymus tea, also known as artichoke tea, is a good remedy for heartburn because it helps reduce acid production in the stomach. This is due to the presence of cynarin, a compound found in artichoke leaves that blocks the production of stomach acid. When you drink artichoke tea, the cynarin absorbs into your bloodstream and travels to your stomach, where it slows down the production of stomach acid. As a result, the stomach produces less acid, which can help ease heartburn symptoms like burning sensations and discomfort. Artichoke tea may also help soothe and calm the digestive system, further reducing heartburn discomfort. Drinking artichoke tea after meals can also help prevent heartburn symptoms from occurring in the first place.

Tannins help to soothe heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and preventing the growth of bacteria that can cause acid reflux.
Saponins are good for heartburn because they help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, which can ease the burning sensation caused by acid reflux.
Quercetin is good for heartburn because it reduces inflammation in the esophagus and protects the mucous membrane from acid damage, helping to prevent the burning sensation associated with heartburn.

Flavonoids, also known as nettle leaf, can help with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, and also by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract to prevent food from coming back up into the esophagus.

If using fresh leaves, simply wash them with water and pat them dry with a clean cloth. If using dried leaves, skip this step. Next, steep one teaspoon of the leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, allowing the tea to infuse. Strain the tea into a separate cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the leaves. Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking. The recommended dosage is one cup per day, ideally after meals to help alleviate heartburn symptoms. It is essential to note that Urtica dioica tea may interact with certain medications, so consult a healthcare professional or a herbalist before using it, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tea, also known as nettle tea, is a natural remedy that may help ease heartburn symptoms. The tea is made from the leaves of the nettle plant, which is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These compounds, called flavonoids and alkaloids, can help soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. When we eat, stomach acid flows up into the esophagus, causing heartburn. The flavonoids in nettle tea may help to calm this acid flow, reducing the burning sensation and discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, nettle tea has a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help to slow down digestion and prevent stomach acid from flowing up into the esophagus.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for heartburn because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the esophagus, making it easier to digest food and relieving symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.
Tannins are good for heartburn because they help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, and their astringent properties help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce symptoms of heartburn.
Polyphenols are good for heartburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can cause heartburn symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal teas for heartburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Urtica dioica herbal teas that help with heartburn, you'll need some fresh or dried nettle leaves. Start by gathering or purchasing about a tablespoon of nettle leaves. If using fresh leaves, rinse them in cold water and pat them dry with a clean cloth. If using dried leaves, skip this step. Next, boil a cup of water in a teapot or kettle. Remove the water from the heat and add the nettle leaves. Allow the leaves to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth. Discard the solids and your nettle tea is ready to drink. To make it more palatable, you can add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice or a bit of honey to taste. Drink the nettle tea 1-2 times a day to help alleviate heartburn symptoms, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.

supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal teas to remedy heartburn is a blend of Foeniculum vulgare (fennel), Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root), and Cynara scolymus (artichoke). Fennel tea helps to ease digestion and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can cause heartburn. Licorice root tea soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the stomach and esophagus, preventing acid from irritating them and causing discomfort. Artichoke tea stimulates bile production, which can help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce symptoms of heartburn. This combination is effective because it addresses multiple causes of heartburn, including digestive issues, inflammation, and acid imbalance. By combining these three herbs, you can create a tea blend that targets heartburn from multiple angles, providing relief and reducing discomfort.
supplementary_avoid: If you have heartburn, there are some herbal teas you should avoid. Peppermint tea is one of them, as it can relax the lower esophageal sphincter, allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus. This can make heartburn symptoms worse.  Another tea to avoid is ginger tea, which can also relax the lower esophageal sphincter and worsen heartburn symptoms.  Additionally, teas made from licorice root should be avoided, as they can increase the production of stomach acid, which can irritate the esophagus and worsen heartburn.  Also, avoid teas made from senna, as they can cause stomach upset and worsen heartburn symptoms.  Furthermore, teas made from dandelion root can also worsen heartburn symptoms, as they can increase the production of stomach acid and relax the lower esophageal sphincter.  Lastly, teas made from angelica root should be avoided, as they can stimulate the stomach and worsen heartburn symptoms.

It has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can help prevent acid reflux. Aloe vera also has a soothing effect on the stomach lining, which can help heal damage caused by acid reflux. This can provide quick relief from heartburn symptoms.  Ginger is another natural remedy for heartburn. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can help prevent acid reflux. Ginger also has a natural muscle relaxant effect, which can help ease stomach contractions that can lead to acid reflux. This can help reduce the frequency and severity of heartburn episodes.  Another remedy is slippery elm, a natural demulcent that can help soothe and protect the stomach lining. It creates a barrier between the stomach acid and the esophagus, which can help prevent acid reflux and heartburn symptoms. Slippery elm is often used in combination with other natural remedies to provide relief from heartburn.  Licorice root is also used to help with heartburn. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can help prevent acid reflux. Licorice root also has a natural muscle relaxant effect, which can help ease stomach contractions that can lead to acid reflux.  Probiotics, which are beneficial bacteria, can also help with heartburn. They help maintain a healthy balance of bacteria in the gut, which can help prevent acid reflux and heartburn symptoms. Probiotics can also help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can help prevent acid reflux.  Apple cider vinegar can help with heartburn by balancing the pH levels in the stomach. It has a natural anti-inflammatory effect that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can help prevent acid reflux. Apple cider vinegar can also help reduce symptoms of acid reflux, such as bloating and discomfort.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have heartburn are often also plagued by acid reflux, which causes stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus. This can lead to a sore throat, difficulty swallowing, and a sensation of food getting stuck in the throat. Some medicinal herbs that can help heal these issues include slippery elm, which soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the throat and esophagus. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and esophagus. Aloe vera juice can help calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, making it easier to swallow food. Dandelion root tea can help stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms. These herbs work by either soothing and protecting the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation, or stimulating digestion to help heal the underlying issues that cause heartburn and acid reflux.
